scope.context_note: Volume III section from an undergraduate real analysis note set. The local mathematical setting is chapter 'Sequences', section 'Cauchy'. The audit goal is in-depth learning of the structure: direct dependencies should be concepts needed to state each node precisely, with common ambient structure carried by the nearest appropriate ancestor.

review_instructions.order: Nodes are topologically sorted so dependencies and ambient ancestors appear before their users when possible.
review_instructions.state: Use reorder/state/working-delta.json as cumulative accepted state across all batches.
review_instructions.checker: Run reorder/tools/check_dependency_edge.py before accepting any add/remove.

nodes.title: Cauchy Sequence
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a
\textbf{Cauchy sequence} if
\[
  \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N
  \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr).
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a
\textbf{Cauchy sequence} if
\[
  \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N
  \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr).
\]

direct_dependencies.title: Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

nodes.title: Absolute Values of Cauchy Sequences
nodes.statement_display: Let $(x_n)$ be a Cauchy real sequence. Then $(|x_n|)$ is Cauchy.
nodes.statement_tex: Let $(x_n)$ be a Cauchy real sequence. Then $(|x_n|)$ is Cauchy.

direct_dependencies.title: Absolute Value Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The \textbf{absolute value sequence} of $(x_n)$ is the real sequence $(|x_n|)$ defined by \[ (|x|)_n := |x_n| \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair

dependencies.title: Relation

nodes.title: Cauchy Criterion via Tails
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy if and only if
for every $\varepsilon>0$ there exists $N\in\mathbb{N}$ such that the
$N$-tail has pairwise term distances less than $\varepsilon$.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy if and only if
for every $\varepsilon>0$ there exists $N\in\mathbb{N}$ such that the
$N$-tail has pairwise term distances less than $\varepsilon$.

direct_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

direct_dependencies.title: $M$-Tail of a Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $M \in \mathbb{N}$. The \textbf{$M$-tail} of $(x_n)$ is the sequence \[ (x_n)_{n \ge M} \;:=\; \bigl(x_M,\, x_{M+1},\, x_{M+2},\, \ldots\bigr). \]

nodes.title: Cauchy Sequence with a Convergent Subsequence
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a Cauchy real sequence. If a subsequence of $(x_n)$
converges to $L\in\mathbb{R}$, then $(x_n)$ converges to $L$.

direct_dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A sequence that converge...

direct_dependencies.title: Subsequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. A \textbf{subsequence} of $(x_n)$ is a sequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$, where $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is a strictly increasing index map.

direct_dependencies.title: Subsequential Limit
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence and let $L\in\mathbb{R}$. The real number $L$ is a \textbf{subsequential limit} of $(x_n)$ if there exists a subsequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})$ such that \[ \lim_{k\to\infty} x_{\sigma(k)}=L. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Distinguished Element Lies in the Underlying Set
Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Closure Under Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $n \in P$, then $S(n) \in P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A sequence that converge...

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strictly Increasing Index Map
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ be a function. The function $\sigma$ is a \textbf{strictly increasing index map} if \[ k<\ell \Longrightarrow \sigma(k)<\sigma(\ell) \qquad \text{for all } k,\ell\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subsequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. A \textbf{subsequence} of $(x_n)$ is a sequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$, where $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is a strictly increasing index map.

transitive_dependencies.title: Subsequential Limit
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence and let $L\in\mathbb{R}$. The real number $L$ is a \textbf{subsequential limit} of $(x_n)$ if there exists a subsequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})$ such that \[ \lim_{k\to\infty} x_{\sigma(k)}=L. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Element Is Either $1$ or a Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $x \in P$, either $x=1$ or there exists $u \in P$ such that $S(u)=x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

ancestor_tree.title: Subsequence
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. A \textbf{subsequence} of $(x_n)$ is a sequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$, where $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is a strictly increasing index map.

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Strictly Increasing Index Map
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ be a function. The function $\sigma$ is a \textbf{strictly increasing index map} if \[ k<\ell \Longrightarrow \sigma(k)<\sigma(\ell) \qquad \text{for all } k,\ell\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System

dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Axiom
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

ancestor_tree.title: Subsequential Limit
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence and let $L\in\mathbb{R}$. The real number $L$ is a \textbf{subsequential limit} of $(x_n)$ if there exists a subsequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})$ such that \[ \lim_{k\to\infty} x_{\sigma(k)}=L. \]

dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A sequence that converge...

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Function
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Strictly Increasing Index Map
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ be a function. The function $\sigma$ is a \textbf{strictly increasing index map} if \[ k<\ell \Longrightarrow \sigma(k)<\sigma(\ell) \qquad \text{for all } k,\ell\in\mathbb{N}. \]

dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

dependencies.title: Every Element Is Either $1$ or a Successor

nodes.title: Cauchy Sequences Are Bounded
nodes.statement_display: Every Cauchy real sequence is bounded.
nodes.statement_tex: Every Cauchy real sequence is bounded.

direct_dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation

dependencies.title: Universal Formula
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

ancestor_tree.title: Cauchy Sequence
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

dependencies.title: Sequence
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

nodes.title: Cauchy Sequences Have Vanishing Successive Differences
nodes.statement_display:
If $(x_n)$ is a Cauchy real sequence, then
\[
  |x_{n+1}-x_n|\to 0.
\]
nodes.statement_tex:
If $(x_n)$ is a Cauchy real sequence, then
\[
  |x_{n+1}-x_n|\to 0.
\]

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable

dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set

nodes.title: Cauchy Tail Diameter Criterion
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy if and only if
every sufficiently late tail has arbitrarily small pairwise diameter.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy if and only if
every sufficiently late tail has arbitrarily small pairwise diameter.

direct_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

direct_dependencies.title: $M$-Tail of a Sequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $M \in \mathbb{N}$. The \textbf{$M$-tail} of $(x_n)$ is the sequence \[ (x_n)_{n \ge M} \;:=\; \bigl(x_M,\, x_{M+1},\, x_{M+2},\, \ldots\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. transitive_dependencies.title: Variable

nodes.title: Convergent Sequences Are Cauchy
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. If $(x_n)$ converges, then $(x_n)$ is
Cauchy.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. If $(x_n)$ converges, then $(x_n)$ is
Cauchy.

A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

ancestor_tree.title: Cauchy Sequence
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. nodes.title: Cauchy Criterion for Real Sequences
nodes.statement_display:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ converges if and only if
$(x_n)$ is Cauchy.
nodes.statement_tex:
Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. Then $(x_n)$ converges if and only if
$(x_n)$ is Cauchy.

direct_dependencies.title: Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem for Sequences
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Every bounded real sequence has a convergent subsequence.

direct_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence with a Convergent Subsequence
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a Cauchy real sequence. If a subsequence of $(x_n)$ converges to $L\in\mathbb{R}$, then $(x_n)$ converges to $L$.

direct_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequences Are Bounded
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Every Cauchy real sequence is bounded.

direct_dependencies.title: Convergent Sequences Are Cauchy
direct_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. If $(x_n)$ converges, then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Distinguished Element Lies in the Underlying Set
Induction Axiom
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A \subseteq P$. If $1 \in A$ and if \[ \forall n \in P\;(n \in A \Longrightarrow S(n) \in A), \] then $A=P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Closure Under Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: If $n \in P$, then $S(n) \in P$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: There exists a Peano system.

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For each $m \in P$, define the function \[ f_m : P \to P \] to be the unique function satisfying \[ f_m(1)=S(m) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(f_m(S(n))=S(f_m(n))). \] For $m,n \in P$, define \[ m+n \coloneqq f_m(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Antisymmetric Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{antisymmetric} on $A$ if any two elements related in both directions must be equal: \[ \forall a,b\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,a)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow a=b. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRa)\rightarrow a=b)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Bounded Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded} exactly when there exists $M > 0$ such that $|x_n| \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is a \textbf{Cauchy sequence} if \[ \forall \varepsilon>0 \;\exists N\in\mathbb{N}\;\forall m,n\ge N \;\bigl(|x_m-x_n|<\varepsilon\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Convergence of a Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}$. The sequence $(x_n)$ \textbf{converges} to $L \in \mathbb{R}$ if \[ \forall \varepsilon > 0 \;\exists N \in \mathbb{N} \;\forall n \ge N \;\bigl(|x_n - L| < \varepsilon\bigr). \] The value $L$ is called the \textbf{limit} of $(x_n)$, written $x_n \to L$ or $\displaystyle\lim_{n \to \infty} x_n = L$. A sequence that converge...

transitive_dependencies.title: Decreasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{decreasing} if \[ x_{n+1} \le x_n \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$ such that: \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)] \item (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$ with $(a,b) \in f$; \item (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$ then $b_1 = b_2$. \end{enumerate}...

transitive_dependencies.title: Increasing Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{increasing} if \[ x_n \le x_{n+1} \qquad \text{for every } n\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Infimum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Data
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $W$ be a set, let $c\in W$, and let $g:W\to W$ be a function. The triple $(W,c,g)$ is called \emph{iterator data} for $(P,S,1)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. A relation $R\subseteq P\times W$ is called an \emph{iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ exactly when \[ (1,c)\in R \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;\forall w\in W\; \bigl((n,w)\in R\Longrightarrow (S(n),g(w))\in R\bigr). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Language
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order language} has two standard readings. As vocabulary, it is the same data as a signature, \[ \mathcal{L}=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}). \] As a formal language generated by a signature $\Sigma$, it is the set of well-formed strings built from the alphabet \[ A=\Sigma\cup V\cup\Lambda, \] where $V$ is a countable s...

transitive_dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Lower Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, $A\subseteq S$, and $l\in S$. The element $l$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $A$ if and only if $l$ is a bound for $A$ from below, meaning every $x\in A$ satisfies $l\le x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. The \emph{minimal iterator relation} for $(W,c,g)$ is \[ R^* = \bigcap\{R\subseteq P\times W : R \text{ is an iterator relation for } (W,c,g)\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monotone Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{monotone} if it is increasing or decreasing.

transitive_dependencies.title: The Natural Numbers
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: The natural numbers $\mathbb{N}$ are the canonical Peano system \[ (\mathbb{N},1,S), \] where $1 \in \mathbb{N}$ is the distinguished base element and $S:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is the successor operation.

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Ordered Set
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Partial Order
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: $R$ is a \emph{partial order} on $A$ if it is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A triple $(P,S,1)$ is called a \emph{Peano system} exactly when $P$ is a set, $1 \in P$, $S : P \to P$ is a function, and the following axioms hold: \begin{enumerate} \item[\textbf{P1.}] $1 \in P$. \item[\textbf{P2.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \in P$. \item[\textbf{P3.}] For every $n \in P$, $S(n) \neq 1$. \item[\textbf{P4.}] For all $m,n \in P$, if $S(m)=...

transitive_dependencies.title: Reflexive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{reflexive} on $A$ if every element of the domain is related to itself: \[ \forall a\in A,\ (a,a)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is the condition $\forall a\in A,\ aRa$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $X$ be a nonempty set. A \textbf{sequence} in $X$ is a function \[ x : \mathbb{N} \to X. \] The value $x(n)$ is written $x_n$ and called the \textbf{$n$-th term} of the sequence. The sequence itself is denoted $(x_n)_{n \in \mathbb{N}}$, or simply $(x_n)$ when the index set is clear.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Above
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded above} exactly when there exists $M \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $x_n \le M$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Sequence Bounded Below
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. The sequence $(x_n)$ is \textbf{bounded below} exactly when there exists $m \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $m \le x_n$ for every $n \in \mathbb{N}$.

transitive_dependencies.title: First-Order Signature
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{first-order signature} is a tuple \[ \Sigma=(\mathcal{C},\mathcal{F},\mathcal{R},\operatorname{ar}), \] where $\mathcal{C}$ is a set of constant symbols, $\mathcal{F}$ is a set of function symbols, $\mathcal{R}$ is a set of relation symbols, and \[ \operatorname{ar}:\mathcal{F}\cup\mathcal{R}\to \mathbb{Z}_{>0} \] assigns to each function or relatio...

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strictly Increasing Index Map
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ be a function. The function $\sigma$ is a \textbf{strictly increasing index map} if \[ k<\ell \Longrightarrow \sigma(k)<\sigma(\ell) \qquad \text{for all } k,\ell\in\mathbb{N}. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subsequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. A \textbf{subsequence} of $(x_n)$ is a sequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})_{k\in\mathbb{N}}$, where $\sigma:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}$ is a strictly increasing index map.

transitive_dependencies.title: Subsequential Limit
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence and let $L\in\mathbb{R}$. The real number $L$ is a \textbf{subsequential limit} of $(x_n)$ if there exists a subsequence $(x_{\sigma(k)})$ such that \[ \lim_{k\to\infty} x_{\sigma(k)}=L. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Subset
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Supremum
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Transitive Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A binary relation $R\subseteq A\times A$ is \emph{transitive} on $A$ if any two composable related pairs collapse to a direct related pair: \[ \forall a,b,c\in A,\; \bigl((a,b)\in R\land(b,c)\in R\bigr)\rightarrow(a,c)\in R. \] In infix notation, this is $\forall a,b,c\in A,\ ((aRb\land bRc)\rightarrow aRc)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Universal Formula
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{universal formula} is a well-formed formula of the form \[ \forall x\,\varphi, \] where \(x\) is a variable and \(\varphi\) is a well-formed formula.

transitive_dependencies.title: Upper Bound
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Variable
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: For a first-order language \(\mathcal L\), a \emph{variable} is a symbol drawn from the distinguished variable stock of \(\mathcal L\). Variables are usually written \[ x,y,z,x_0,x_1,x_2,\ldots . \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Existence of an Iterator Function
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Forced Values Are Unique
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. If $(n,w_0)\in R^*$ and $(n,w_1)\in R^*$, then $w_0=w_1$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Consistency of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$. Then $R^*$ is an iterator relation for $(W,c,g)$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Completeness of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there exists $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Determinism of the Minimal Iterator Relation
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it, and let $R^*$ be the minimal iterator relation. For each $n\in P$, there is at most one $w\in W$ such that $(n,w)\in R^*$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Uniqueness of Iterator Functions
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. If $f,h:P\to W$ satisfy \[ f(1)=c,\qquad h(1)=c, \] and \[ \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(h(S(n))=g(h(n))), \] then $f=h$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem for Sequences
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Every bounded real sequence has a convergent subsequence.

transitive_dependencies.title: Bounded Monotone Sequence Equivalences
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing, then $(x_n)$ converges if and only if $(x_n)$ is bounded above. \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing, then $(x_n)$ converges if and only if $(x_n)$ is bounded below. \item If $(x_n)$ is monotone, then $(x_n)$ converges if and only if $(x_n)$ is bounded. \end{enumerat...

transitive_dependencies.title: Boundedness Passes to Subsequences
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Every subsequence of a bounded real sequence is bounded.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequence with a Convergent Subsequence
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a Cauchy real sequence. If a subsequence of $(x_n)$ converges to $L\in\mathbb{R}$, then $(x_n)$ converges to $L$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Cauchy Sequences Are Bounded
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Every Cauchy real sequence is bounded.

transitive_dependencies.title: Convergent Sequences Are Cauchy
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. If $(x_n)$ converges, then $(x_n)$ is Cauchy.

transitive_dependencies.title: Every Element Is Either $1$ or a Successor
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $x \in P$, either $x=1$ or there exists $u \in P$ such that $S(u)=x$.

transitive_dependencies.title: Induction Principle for a Peano System
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $\varphi$ be a predicate on $P$. If \[ \varphi(1) \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n \in P\;(\varphi(n) \Longrightarrow \varphi(S(n))), \] then \[ \forall n \in P\;\varphi(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Monotone Convergence Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(x_n)$ be a real sequence. \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)] \item If $(x_n)$ is increasing and bounded above, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\sup\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \item If $(x_n)$ is decreasing and bounded below, then $(x_n)$ converges and \[ \lim_{n\to\infty}x_n=\inf\{x_n:n\in\mathbb{N}\}. \] \end{enumerate}

transitive_dependencies.title: Monotone Subsequence Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Every real sequence has a monotone subsequence.

transitive_dependencies.title: One Plus $n$
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $n \in P$, \[ 1+n=S(n). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Peano Iterator Theorem
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $(W,c,g)$ be iterator data for it. Then there exists a unique function $f:P\to W$ such that \[ f(1)=c \qquad\text{and}\qquad \forall n\in P\;(f(S(n))=g(f(n))). \]

transitive_dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
transitive_dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

ancestor_tree.title: Bolzano-Weierstrass Theorem for Sequences
ancestor_tree.statement_preview: Every bounded real sequence has a convergent subsequence.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Pairing
dependencies.statement_preview: For any two sets, there exists a set containing exactly those two sets. \[ \forall A \, \forall B \, \exists C \, \forall x \, (x \in C \leftrightarrow (x = A \lor x = B)). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom of Power Set
dependencies.statement_preview: For any set, there exists a set of all its subsets. \[ \forall A \, \exists P \, \forall x \, (x \in P \leftrightarrow x \subseteq A). \]

dependencies.title: Axiom Schema of Separation
dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Ordered Pair
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $a$ and $b$ be sets. The \emph{ordered pair} $(a,b)$ is defined (Kuratowski) as \[ (a,b) \;:=\; \{\{a\}, \{a,b\}\}. \]

dependencies.title: Relation
dependencies.statement_preview: A \emph{relation} from $A$ to $B$ is any subset $R \subseteq A \times B$. If $(a,b) \in R$ we write $a \, R \, b$ and say ``$a$ is related to $b$.'' When $A = B$ we say $R$ is a \emph{relation on $A$}.

dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
dependencies.statement_preview: The \emph{Cartesian product} of sets $A$ and $B$ is: \[ A \times B \;:=\; \{\, (a,b) \mid a \in A \text{ and } b \in B \,\}. \]

dependencies.title: Infimum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $i\in S$. The element $i$ is the infimum of $A$ if and only if $i$ is a lower bound of $A$ and every lower bound $l$ of $A$ satisfies $l\le i$.

dependencies.title: Least Upper Bound Property
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set. The set $S$ has the \emph{Least Upper Bound Property} if and only if every nonempty subset of $S$ that is bounded above in $S$ has a supremum in $S$.

dependencies.title: Ordered Set
dependencies.statement_preview: An \emph{ordered set} (or \emph{partially ordered set}, \emph{poset}) is a pair $(A,\leq)$ where $\leq$ is a partial order on $A$: a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.

dependencies.title: Subset
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. We say $A$ is a \emph{subset} of $B$, written $A \subseteq B$, if every element of $A$ is also an element of $B$: \[ A \subseteq B \;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\; \forall x \, (x \in A \rightarrow x \in B). \]

dependencies.title: Supremum
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A \subseteq S$, and let $s \in S$. The element $s$ is the supremum of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and every upper bound $u$ of $A$ satisfies $s \le u$.

dependencies.title: Upper Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $u\in S$. The element $u$ is an \emph{upper bound} of $A$ if and only if $u$ is a bound for $A$ from above, meaning $x\le u$ for every $x\in A$.

dependencies.title: Lower Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, $A\subseteq S$, and $l\in S$. The element $l$ is a \emph{lower bound} of $A$ if and only if $l$ is a bound for $A$ from below, meaning every $x\in A$ satisfies $l\le x$.

dependencies.title: Bound
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(S,\le)$ be an ordered set, let $A\subseteq S$, and let $b\in S$. The element $b$ is a \emph{bound} for $A$ if and only if $b$ is a uniform one-sided comparison point for all elements of $A$: \[ \operatorname{Bound}(b,A) \Longleftrightarrow \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(x\le b)\bigr] \lor \bigl[\forall x\in A\;(b\le x)\bigr]. \]

dependencies.statement_preview: Given a set and a property, there exists a subset containing exactly the elements satisfying that property. For any formula $\varphi(x)$, \[ \forall A \, \exists B \, \forall x \, (x \in B \leftrightarrow (x \in A \land \varphi(x))). \]

dependencies.title: Universal Formula

dependencies.title: Strict Order Successor Characterization
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a<b \Longleftrightarrow S(a) \le b. \]

dependencies.title: Less Than or Equal on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a \le b \] exactly when \[ a<b \qquad\text{or}\qquad a=b. \]

dependencies.title: Strict Less Than on a Peano System
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system, and let $a,b \in P$. One writes \[ a<b \] exactly when there exists $c \in P$ such that \[ b=a+c. \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Associative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b,c \in P$, \[ (a+b)+c=a+(b+c). \]

dependencies.title: Addition Is Commutative
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $a,b \in P$, \[ a+b=b+a. \]

dependencies.title: Addition Successor on the Right
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m,n \in P$, \[ m+S(n)=S(m+n). \]

dependencies.title: Addition With $1$
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $m \in P$, \[ m+1=S(m). \]

dependencies.title: Every Element Is Either $1$ or a Successor
dependencies.statement_preview: Let $(P,S,1)$ be a Peano system. For every $x \in P$, either $x=1$ or there exists $u \in P$ such that $S(u)=x$.
